back_translation: When Jesus had been (royal) born at the town of Bethlehem in the region of Judea during the reign of King Herod, learned-astronomers from the east traveled to the city of Jerusalem.
They asked: «Where is the newly-(royal)-born king of the Jewish people? Because we have seen his (royal) star rising in the east, and so we have come to worship him (royal).»
When King Herod heard this, he was deeply troubled, and all the people of Jerusalem became troubled with him as well.
When he (royal) had summoned all the chief priests and scribes of the Jewish people for consultation, he inquired of them where the Christ would (royal) be born.
They answered him: «At the town of Bethlehem in the region of Judea, because it is written through the prophet thus:

‹And you, Bethlehem, land of Judah, are by no means the least-lowly of all the leading towns of Judah; for out of you will come forth a ruler who will care-for the people-nation of Israel, which is ours, as a shepherd tends sheep.›»
Then Herod summoned the learned-astronomers secretly and inquired of them in precise detail about the time when that star had appeared.
Herod then sent them to Bethlehem, saying: «Go and search thoroughly for the (royal) child; when you have found him, come and report to me, so that I may also go to worship him (royal) likewise.»

When they had heard the king, they set out on their journey; and behold, the star they had seen rising in the east went ahead of them until it came to the area above the place where the (royal) child was (royal) staying; then it stopped above that place.
When they saw that star, they had exceedingly great joy.

When they entered the house, they saw the (royal) child with Mary his (royal) mother. They prostrated themselves and worshiped him (royal), and they opened their treasure-chests and offered-up gifts to him (royal): gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
When God (royally) warned them in a dream not to return to visit Herod, they traveled back to their own land by another route.

When the learned-astronomers had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord (royal, full form) appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying: «Arise and take the (royal) child and his (royal) mother and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to search for the (royal) child to kill him unto death.»
Joseph then arose and took the (royal) child and his (royal) mother and traveled to Egypt that very night.
His family stayed there until Herod ended-his-life, in order to fulfill what the Lord (royal, full form) (royally) spoke through the prophet, saying: «I called my son out of Egypt.»

When Herod realized that he had been deceived by the learned-astronomers, he became enraged; and he sent men to execute all the boy-children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding vicinity, from two years old and under, according to the time that Herod had inquired about precisely from the learned-astronomers.
At that time was fulfilled what was (royally) spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying:

«A voice was heard in the town of Ramah, the sound of weeping and the sound of loud lamentation; Rachel was weeping for her children, and she refused to accept comforting, because the children were no more.»
When Herod had ended-his-life, behold, an angel of the Lord (royal, full form) appeared to Joseph in a dream in Egypt,
saying: «Arise and take the (royal) child and his (royal) mother back to the land of Israel, because those who would take the life of the (royal) child have all died.»
Joseph then arose and took the (royal) child and his (royal) mother back into the land of Israel.

But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning in the region of Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there; and when God (royally) warned him in a dream, he traveled to the region of Galilee,
and when he arrived, he settled in a town named Nazareth, in order to fulfill what was (royally) spoken through the prophets, saying: «He (royal) will be called a Nazarene.»
